question: What is the nominal volume of the Hot Leg?

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across different power levels by establishing steady-state parameters for both primary and secondary systems, such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures. These parameters are calculated using thermal hydraulic software and verified by hand, with uncertainties and design margins considered to ensure efficient operation and safety. The design supports natural circulation with low pressure losses, and uncertainties like SG tube plugging do not affect primary coolant flow rate determination.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering unnecessary reactor trips or safety system actuations. Instrumentation provides continuous monitoring of pressure and water level to support safe operation.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity. The RCS interfaces with several other systems, including chemical control, emergency cooling, and control rod drives.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including low alloy steel for the baffle plate with stainless steel and nickel-based cladding for corrosion protection, and specifies the use of corrosion-resistant alloys for heater bundles, cover plates, and bolting. All materials are selected for compatibility with operating environments and comply with relevant BPVC Section II specifications.

edges.description: Section A discusses the establishment of steady state values for primary system parameters, which would include the nominal volume of the Hot Leg, while Section B provides a general description of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) but does not specify the Hot Leg volume.
Section A references Section B for further description of the steam generators (SGs), indicating that details relevant to system evaluation, such as component volumes, may be found in Section B.

Section A describes the pressurizer as a component of the reactor vessel and its connection to the reactor coolant system (RCS), while Section B provides a system-level overview of the RCS and its interaction with the pressurizer, specifically mentioning the removal of gases from the pressurizer volume; thus, Section A provides detailed component information that specifies the general system description in Section B.

Section A describes the function and configuration of the pressurizer, while Section B provides references to other sections for material specifications of pressurizer components, connecting them by detailing where to find further information about the components mentioned in Section A.

Section A provides a general system description of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) and its components, while Section B gives specific details about the pressurizer materials and construction, with both sections referencing the RPV and its components but neither directly providing the nominal volume of the Hot Leg.
